tldr: 43°C and extreme fire danger forced organisers to cut Willunga Hill from the route, shortening the stage from 176 km to 130.8 km and turning the canonical mountain finish into an uphill-drag bunch sprint. Ethan Vernon won, Lund Andresen second, Pithie third.

narrative.headline: Heat kills the mountain stage, Vernon wins the substitute
narrative.body:
Pre-race forecasts of 43°C and active fire danger forced ASO and Tour Down Under organisers to remove Willunga Hill from the route. The shortened 130.8 km stage finished on Willunga township's High Street in an uphill drag sprint. Vernon's NSN sprint was the only kick that mattered.

The GC stayed exactly where it had been after Stage 2 — Vine in the leader's jersey, the mountain test that was meant to define the week now postponed by climate.

gcImpact.headline: Stage cut by heat, GC unchanged
gcImpact.body: Vine retains the leader's jersey, Schmid still second, Sweeny third. Willunga's removal hands him a defending day.

controversies.description: Willunga Hill removed from stage route because of 43°C forecast and active fire danger; stage shortened from 176 km to 130.8 km
